issues.message: Filter inputs (e.g. callsign text field, year/state/EF-rating checkbox groups) appear to lack programmatic labels — extractor flagged missing_form_label and missing_accessible_name.
Callsign search input lacks an associated label; extractor flagged missing_form_label / missing_accessible_name.
Large blank region (~37.9% sparsity, ~642k px area below the 12-row table) — the log table only renders one page worth of rows then leaves the lower viewport empty.
Three of eight gear cards in the mobile-mesonet grid have mismatched internal hierarchy/tag structure compared to the majority pattern (node-95, node-146, node-218).
The 12 storm-feature filter-chip / thumbnail glyphs show inconsistent size and underlying shape across the group.
Layout consistency score is 48 — calendar grid has 28 alignment errors out of 64 elements (row-bottom alignment and vertical-alignment groups).

repair_instructions: On /logs, the page currently renders only ~12 visible rows and then leaves a ~530px-tall blank band beneath the table. Either (a) increase the rows-per-page so the table fills the viewport down to the pagination control, or (b) move ancillary content (export controls, schema notes, last-modified strip, or a mini chart summarizing record counts by year/EF) into that blank region so the page reads as dense rather than truncated.

Add explicit <label> elements (or aria-label/aria-labelledby) to the form controls on /logs and /roster. Specifically: the callsign text input on /logs (placeholder 'e.g. KC5-WHIRL'), the callsign search input on /roster (placeholder 'search callsign / CWA / vehicle ID…'), and each filter checkbox group (Year, State/CWA, EF-Rating, Storm Mode) should have a programmatically associated label or aria-labelledby pointing at the section heading.

On /gear, normalize the internal structure of every card in the mobile-mesonet grid. The majority pattern is: icon block → <h3> title → body paragraph → spec rows → COMPARE button. Three cards (the ones flagged as node-95, node-146, node-218 — these appear to be cards with the DIY/LEG badges and price ranges that differ from the FIELD/CERT majority) have a different tag hierarchy. Refactor each card to use identical wrapper tags, heading levels, and spec-row markup so they look and parse uniformly.

On /gallery, give every storm-feature thumbnail/filter glyph the same bounding box and underlying SVG shape conventions. Right now the supercell silhouettes vary in height/aspect across the 12 visible tiles (some are deep classic bells, some are flatter HP wedges, some show mammatus rows or lightning bolts at very different scales). Either standardize the artboard size and stroke weight across all SVG thumbnails, or wrap each thumbnail in a fixed-aspect container so they line up as a coherent grid.

On /calendar, tighten the day-cell alignment. The current grid has 28 alignment errors across 64 elements — most stem from inconsistent vertical placement of the day-number and risk-chip inside cells with different content lengths. Use a consistent flexbox or grid layout per cell (e.g. day number top-left at a fixed offset, risk chip bottom-left at a fixed offset) so the chips and numbers line up across rows regardless of whether the cell carries an MRGL/SLGT/ENH/MOD/HIGH tag.
